items.title: 'I was in tears': Living without a heater through Canberra's winter

children.content: Barbara Belmonte, an ACT public housing resident, has been waiting weeks for her heater to be repaired amid the sub-zero temperatures of a Canberra winter — and she's not the only one.

gallery.altText: Barbara Belmont, public housing resident, sits and gestures while talking.

thumbnail.altText: Barbara Belmont, public housing resident, sits and gestures while talking.

items.title: New AI-powered alert system to warn drivers of approaching wildlife

children.content: An estimated 10 million animals are killed on Australian roads each year. It's hoped a new artificial intelligence-powered alert system being trialled on the Monaro Highway near Canberra will reduce the toll.

image.altText: A wildlife sign with a digital panel at the bottom.

items.title: 'We didn't give up': Afghan women's refugee cricketers celebrate ICC decision

children.content: Five years since fleeing Afghanistan after the Taliban returned to power, the country's women's refugee cricket team has secured a pathway to compete at the international level.

image.altText: Benafsha Hashimi, Afghanistan Women's Cricket Team

items.title: Home Affairs asks DV survivors to prove abuser relationship 'genuine'

children.content: A federal government safety net has been identified as one of the biggest stressors for migrant women experiencing domestic and family violence.

gallery.altText: An anonymous woman in the city, back of head

thumbnail.altText: An anonymous woman in the city, back of head

items.title: 'I didn't feel safe': Mum reveals concerns before childcare closure

children.content: A Canberra mother had concerns for her daughter's safety at Edge Early Learning in Higgins long before the ACT regulator ordered the centre to close.

image.altText: A mother stands with a serious expression with her two children.

items.title: Canberra United FC finally has a buyer. Who are the new owners?

children.content: The new owners of the national capital's A-League Women side have pointed to the launch of a men's team.

image.altText: Football teammates celebrating.

items.title: These lemurs like to 'move it, move it' to the sound of a harp

children.content: Harp therapist Alison Ware provides musical enrichment for animals at Canberra's National Zoo and Aquarium, adapting what she plays to suit different species.

gallery.altText: A woman playing a harp beside a lemur.

thumbnail.altText: A woman playing a harp beside a lemur.

items.title: Mother of identical quadruplets calls them her 'miracle babies'

children.content: A maternal foetal medicine expert believes identical quadruplets born in Brisbane may be even rarer than one in 15 million, given they shared a placenta.

gallery.altText: A newborn baby holds onto her mother's finger on a white blanket with a pink heart.

thumbnail.altText: A newborn baby holds onto her mother's finger on a white blanket with a pink heart.

items.title: Synagogue had requests for security funding rejected before firebombing

children.content: Jewish leaders have given evidence to the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion regarding security concerns for synagogues nationwide.

image.altText: Firefights walk past fire damage and smashed glass at the Synagogue.

items.title: Surgeon banned after botched bowel op contributes to patient's death

children.content: A six-month ban on Dr Liu-Ming Schmidt, who works across multiple hospitals in southern NSW, comes after 13 patient complaints including a missed cancer diagnosis and a "catastrophic error" that contributed to a patient's death.

image.altText: A woman walking along a pathway

items.title: US Army veteran arrested after FBI building 'attacked' during ICE protest

children.content: A former US Army soldier protesting Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) has been arrested in New York after allegedly setting off fireworks, lighting a fire and shooting a pellet gun outside a federal office building.

articles.title: Diplomatic efforts to keep US from cutting ties with UNHCR begin, sources say

children.content: The Trump administration is said to be weighing cutting ties with the UN refugee agency, prompting a campaign for the US to maintain support.

image.altText: A white banner includes a logo showing a person seeking shelter under a pair of open hands.

articles.title: US to impose 50pc tariffs on most Canadian goods

children.content: The White House says the tariffs will go into effect in 30 days, meaning there is time for negotiations.

image.altText: A bottleshop is shown with a sign saying 'we are no longer selling liquor from the USA' on a shelf of red wine bottles

gallery.altText: A fire producing dark black smoke rises from a NY footpath

thumbnail.altText: A fire producing dark black smoke rises from a NY footpath

items.title: India steps up response to deadly US, Iran ship strikes

children.content: The Indian government is taking action after 10 sailors were killed and dozens more injured in attacks on commercial cargo ships and oil tankers during the battle for control of the Strait of Hormuz.

image.altText: An Indian boy sits while wearing a stripe shirt.

items.title: China, Philippines blame each other over South China Sea clash

children.content: Vision posted online by the Philippines Coast Guard shows Filipino and Chinese personnel briefly engaging each other from inflatable boats, with the Philippines accusing China of assaulting one of its navy staff.

image.altText: An image shows inflatable boats with personnel from the Philippine Navy clashing with personnel from China's Coast Guard.

items.title: Nine to cut jobs from metro newspaper newsrooms, ASX finishes flat — as it happened

children.content: Nine Entertainment shares were steady following news of redundancies at publications including The Age, The Sydney Morning Herald, Brisbane Times and WAtoday. On the ASX, the share market has finished flat after a negative session on Wall Street.

image.altText: The outside of the Nine Entertainment headquarters in North Sydney.

items.title: Anthony Albanese on the night in parliament he would rather not relive

children.content: From left-wing roots to "radical centre" prime minister, Anthony Albanese reflects on his 30-year career in Parliament House.

image.altText: Annabel Crabb and Anthony Albanese stand smiling on the porch of an old building

items.title: Kids do feel the cold. So why won't they wear a jumper?

children.content: Do kids really not feel the cold like adults do? Or are they just expressing their independence? And when should you insist?

items.title: Ian doesn't want children. He's part of a growing trend among men

children.content: About 15 per cent of Australian men say they don't want children, an increase from 11 per cent in 2005. Ian says his own childhood played a large part in his decision.

image.altText: Ian Hendry with dog Maisy at the beach

items.title: Why 'narcissist' bosses are more likely to oppose work from home

children.content: Research published last month found narcissistic leaders — those with a tendency to be self-centred and entitled — were more likely than others to resist remote work. Why? The study concluded "it interferes with their motivations for power and status".

image.altText: a man gesturing as he sits in front of a laptop

items.title: Police probe use of force on handcuffed teen injured during Perth arrest

children.content: A handcuffed teenager was injured when he was forced to the ground by a police officer, sparking a WA Police review after video of the incident emerged.

items.title: PMOS/PCOS linked to 'significantly higher' stroke, heart attack risk

children.content: Women with polyendocrine metabolic ovarian syndrome (PMOS, formerly PCOS) have a "significantly higher" risk of cardiovascular disease than those without, a new study suggests.

image.altText: Practitioner is examining patient checking blood pressure and pulsethen talking to young woman during medical visit.

items.title: Stubborn stigma fuelling shocking violence against sex workers in Australia

children.content: A new inquiry into violence against sex workers in Australia and Thailand comes just weeks after the murder of Thai teenager Tunchanok Donhomla, allegedly by an Australian man.

image.altText: a woman carries a sign saying 'stop mythologising men who kill sex workers'

items.title: Australian EV owners deal with 'range anxiety'. China has almost eliminated it

children.content: You can swap an empty electric vehicle battery in about four minutes in China. It is part of how China has come to dominate the EV industry.

gallery.altText: A young boy poking his head into the open passenger door of a vehicle with yellow seat covers

thumbnail.altText: A young boy poking his head into the open passenger door of a vehicle with yellow seat covers

items.title: Randa Abdel-Fattah appears to abandon threatened defamation case against SA Premier

children.content: Earlier this year, Palestinian-Australian author Randa Abdel-Fattah said she had sent two concerns notices to SA Premier Peter Malinauskas over comments made around the time of her controversial removal from the line-up of this year's Adelaide Writers' Week.

image.altText: A woman with long brown hair, wearing a black embroidered jacket, with her hands clasped together.

items.title: Queensland government advertising costs taxpayers almost $50 million

children.content: Parliamentary budget estimates revealed the LNP administration spent more than $5 million on a statewide campaign on what it is delivering for Queensland.

image.altText: a billboard saying 50 cent fares made permanent

items.title: Man charged as police crack down on alleged criminal syndicate

children.content: Victoria Police say they will be targeting anyone who organised or assisted with an alleged drug and money laundering operation for a state-wide criminal syndicate.

image.altText: Man gets put into police car under arrest

items.title: Man found not guilty of murder with scooter during street fight

children.content: A jury finds that Kane Flanders acted in self-defence when he struck Ash Morgan over the head with a Razor scooter at Raymond Terrace in 2024.

image.altText: Man in cap and singlet.

items.title: 'Happening to everybody': AI-manipulated images exploited by scammers

children.content: Papua New Guinea prepares new laws to criminalise the malicious use of artificial intelligence, including deepfakes, voice cloning and digital impersonation.

gallery.altText: An AI-generated image showing Jennifer Baing with a group of people holding cash standing behind another pile of cash.

thumbnail.altText: An AI-generated image showing Jennifer Baing with a group of people holding cash standing behind another pile of cash.

items.title: Medical clinic waited months to tell patients of data breach

children.content: Patients at a medical clinic in Brisbane's north only found out about the data breach almost three months after it was targeted by hackers.

image.altText: a health clinic exterior with a sunset behind it

items.title: Vaughters says night tests would have saved cycling '25 years of drama'

children.content: A former cyclist who took performance-enhancing drugs, now a team boss at the Tour de France, supports doping tests in the middle of the night at cycling's biggest race.

image.altText: A former pro cyclist speaks into a microphone at a press conference for a documentary at a film festival.

items.title: How this Champions League winner aims to ignite Stingers

children.content: Winning the Champions League with Greek giants Olympiacos was "one of the coolest moments" of Abby Andrews's life. She wants to use that experience to push the Stingers to the next level.

image.altText: Abby Andrews smiles

items.title: Weatherald retains Australian Test spot despite middling Ashes returns

children.content: Jake Weatherald is retained in Australia's 13-player Test squad for the upcoming series against Bangladesh.

image.altText: Australia batter Jake Weatherald plays a cover drive while down on one knee during a Test.

items.title: Tiny 'desert' fish found only in Australia narrowly avoids extinction

children.content: With scoops made from milk bottles, volunteers have been working tirelessly to save the red-fin blue-eye, found nowhere else in the world, from extinction.

gallery.altText: man holding up clear container with small fish in it

thumbnail.altText: man holding up clear container with small fish in it

items.title: Andy Burnham becomes British PM, provides AUKUS hints in first speech

children.content: Andy Burnham has pledged to provide immediate cost-of-living relief for Brits and invited US President Donald Trump to Manchester in the hours after becoming UK PM on Monday.

image.altText: A man in a suit speaking.

items.title: Tate brothers face court for first time since Miami arrest

children.content: Andrew and Tristan Tate's attorney says an attempt to extradite the brothers following their arrest in Miami is politically motivated.

image.altText: Tristan and Andrew Tate pictured sitting down in the audience at an event with serious expressions

items.title: 'Our friends': How a nationwide Bug Hunt galvanized citizen scientists

children.content: More than 1 million observations of insects, spiders, snails and other invertebrates have been recorded by citizen scientists as part of Bug Hunt Australia since October 2025.

gallery.altText: Woman in broad-brimmed hat with a small black beetle on her finger.
A fly up close on a white and green flower.
Isabel and mum erica stand outdoors they are citizen scientists who often take part in the bug hunt

thumbnail.altText: Woman in broad-brimmed hat with a small black beetle on her finger.
